data.narrative:
Atom Bodies is a Series A biotech company that has raised $2M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 11 peers across Biotech & Health: 3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage firms including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $2M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $4M. Peer-median anchoring examines six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, which show valuations ranging from $100M to $3.3B with a median of $805M. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied, as Atom Bodies does not publicly disclose revenue figures.

The two applicable methods produce a wide triangulated range: $3M on the low end (stage-progression floor), $404M at the median, and $926M on the high end (peer median plus adjustment buffer). The dispersion reflects material uncertainty about Atom Bodies' development progress, IP position, and clinical/commercial traction relative to the peer set. Near-term clarity on revenue generation, pipeline advancement, or competitive differentiation would narrow this range materially. In the absence of such data, valuations in the $4M–$100M band align with early-stage positioning, while higher peer comparables ($600M+) assume substantially de-risked milestones not yet evident in available filings.
